mission.title: fluent-bit: Failed to flush chunk engine error on Kafka output plugin after recover
mission.description: Failed to flush chunk engine error on Kafka output plugin after recover. This issue affects 22+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluent-bit troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
## Bug Report

Our fluentBit fails to flush chunks to Kafka output plugin after Kafka cluster is recover from downtime.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Failed to flush chunk engine error on Kafka output plugin…
steps.description:
If Kafka is down, our Kafka output connector (based on librdkafka) won't be able to deliver the records, hence every time the engine passes records to the plugin will be enqueued by librdkafka. If the message cannot be enqueued in librdkafka, Fluent Bit will retry later.

If librdkafka queue is not
```yaml
[SERVICE]
    Flush        5
    Daemon       Off
    Log_Level    ${LOG_LEVEL}

    Parsers_File parsers.conf
    Plugins_File plugins.conf

    HTTP_Server  On
    HTTP_Listen  0.0.0.0
    HTTP_Port    2020

[INPUT]
    Name   dummy
    Rate   1
    Tag    dummy.log

[OUTPUT]
    Name  stdout
    Match *

[OUTPUT]
    Name                           kafka
    Match                          *
    Brokers                        ${BROKER_ADDRESS}
    Topics                         bit
    Timestamp_Key  @timestamp
    Retry_Limit    false
    # Specify the number of extra seconds to monitor a fi
```

steps.title: Upgrade fluent-bit to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade fluent-bit:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent-bit/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit
helm list -n fluent-bit
```

steps.title: Confirm Failed to flush chunk engine error on Kafka… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
If Kafka is down, our Kafka output connector (based on librdkafka) won't be able to deliver the records, hence every time the engine passes records to the plugin will be enqueued by librdkafka. If the message cannot be enqueued in librdkafka, Fluent Bit will retry later.

If librdkafka queue is not flushed you will face this issue and is expected.
codeSnippets:
[SERVICE]
    Flush        5
    Daemon       Off
    Log_Level    ${LOG_LEVEL}

    Parsers_File parsers.conf
    Plugins_File plugins.conf

    HTTP_Server  On
    HTTP_Listen  0.0.0.0
    HTTP_Port    2020

[INPUT]
    Name   dummy
    Rate   1
    Tag    dummy.log

[OUTPUT]
    Name  stdout
    Match *

[OUTPUT]
    Name                           kafka
    Match                          *
    Brokers                        ${BROKER_ADDRESS}
    Topics                         bit
    Timestamp_Key  @timestamp
    Retry_Limit    false
    # Specify the number of extra seconds to monitor a file once is rotated in case some pending data is flushed
    Rotate_Wait    20
    rdkafka.queue.buffering.max.ms ${KAFKA_QUEUE_BUFFER_MAX_MS}
    # for logs you'll probably want this to be 0 or 1, not

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
